metric_name: AS/400 vs. NT Server competitive result (1996)
metric_value: AS/400 won hands down vs. NT Server with BackOffice in reliability, scalability, supportability, total cost of acquisition and maintenance, MIS staff costs, ROI, and probable business disruption

metric_name: NT Server 4.0 on Integrated PC Server availability timeline
metric_value: NT Server 4.0 scheduled for general availability on AS/400 Integrated PC Server during Q1 1998

metric_name: NT Server on AS/400 delivery outcome
metric_value: NT Server 4.0 was delivered on the AS/400 Integrated PC Server as planned in early 1998; IBM fulfilled commitment
notes: IBM confirmed delivery per Aberdeen's reported timeline

metric_name: Integrated PC Server hardware specifications
metric_value: Intel Pentium Pro 200MHz processor board with 512MB memory; fits AS/400 slot; disk/tape/I/O provided by AS/400; originally named FSIOP

metric_name: Integrated PC Server performance impact on AS/400
metric_value: Users report insignificant degradation in AS/400 production application performance when NOS runs on Integrated PC Server — surpasses expected negative result
notes: Key technical finding endorsing the architecture

metric_name: NT Server integration limits vs. LAN Server/400 and NetWare
metric_value: NT Server not as tightly integrated with OS/400 as LAN Server/400 and NetWare; requires own operator console; cannot use AS/400 console
notes: Due to Microsoft policy: NT must be NT regardless of hardware platform

metric_name: Microsoft NT Server modification policy
metric_value: Microsoft corporate policy: will not modify NT Server for any hardware manufacturer — NT must be NT regardless of platform
notes: Limits integration depth; constrains IBM's customization

metric_name: OS/400 and NT Server integration points
metric_value: Three integration points: ODBC, common user profiles, OS/400 and NT file system integration; print integration in next OS/400 release

metric_name: Lotus Domino 4.5 native AS/400 availability
metric_value: Domino 4.5 available on Integrated PC Server May 1997; native mode on AS/400 expected year-end 1997

metric_name: IBM AS/400 target market for NT integration
metric_value: Primary target: satellite offices with small number of users; AS/400 for production applications, NT for file/print NOS; eliminates need for separate PC server

metric_name: AS/400 superiority for transaction processing vs. NT BackOffice
metric_value: AS/400 has more robust production qualities (reliability, serviceability, availability, security) required for transaction processing; BackOffice on NT not recommended for transaction workloads
notes: Aberdeen explicitly advises against BackOffice for transaction processing on AS/400

metric_name: Integrated PC Server architectural uniqueness
metric_value: Unique in server mid-range: no other major supplier designed a system where separate processors handle enterprise production apps vs. file/print services in the same cabinet
notes: Aberdeen credits IBM creativity and ingenuity

metric_name: AS/400 long-term platform viability
metric_value: AS/400 will continue to be the production application platform of choice; NT will not replace it for business-critical workloads
notes: Aberdeen prediction against NT replacing AS/400

metric_name: AS/400 platform long-term viability outcome
metric_value: AS/400 survived and evolved: renamed iSeries (2000), System i, then IBM i; still active as IBM Power Systems in 2025 with active installed base globally; Aberdeen's prediction confirmed
notes: Verified via multiple 2024-2025 sources on IBM i/Power Systems active status

metric_name: IBM Network Computing vision (AS/400 + NT + Network Stations)
metric_value: Combination of AS/400, NT, Office for NT/SmartSuite, and Network Stations will deliver real Network Computing benefits described by IBM senior executives
notes: Aberdeen endorses IBM's network computing vision

metric_name: IBM Network Station outcome
metric_value: IBM Network Station was discontinued by 2001 as thin-client market failed to materialize at predicted scale; Network Computing vision partially delivered but Network Stations themselves were market failures
notes: Network Computing concept survived in different forms (cloud, VDI); specific Network Station product line was discontinued

metric_name: AS/400 evolutionary history
metric_value: AS/400 has 20+ year history; started as System/3; major architectural changes resulted in product name changes through progressively newer models

metric_name: NOS options for AS/400 Integrated PC Server
metric_value: Three NOS options: Novell NetWare, IBM LAN Server/400, and Microsoft NT Server; IBM proves ability to open AS/400 to customer requirements
notes: Flexibility vs. proprietary lock-in argument resolved in favor of openness

metric_name: IBM MIS cost reduction through AS/400+NT integration
metric_value: MIS can manage relatively complex user environment (AS/400 + NT + intranet) at significantly lower cost than today's separate PC LAN and AS/400 operations

metric_name: DB2/400 to NT application data transfer
metric_value: Users can transfer data from DB2/400 to NT applications via ODBC; useful for mass mailings, customized presentations from customer databases
notes: Practical use case for ODBC integration
